title: Rebuild 10 in Dallas March 26 April 2 2009
wikitext:
[]: Rebuild 1.0 in Dallas (March 26 - April 2, 2009)             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 16, 2009 7:04 pm http://www.evageeks.org/2009/02/evangelion-10-showing-at-afi-dallas-2009/ People in the Dallas area (or even Texas) should try and check it out on the big screen.

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Mon Feb 16, 2009 7:26 pm If there's some way I can get there and fund it I'm going. Dallas is only a few hours from Houston. It'd be worth it for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Mon Feb 16, 2009 8:04 pm Why can't those fuckers bring it to a state that actually matters?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Feb 16, 2009 9:17 pm BrikHaus wrote: Why can't those fuckers bring it to a state that actually matters?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Feb 16, 2009 9:46 pm Sigh. Bring it to Virginia, or New York, Boston. Somewhere. Oh well, there's 9 months until the DVD release (why?) so maybe I'll get lucky and get a chance to see it on a big screen. Of course we are talking Film Projections right? And not just a DVD on a big screen? Otherwise it's pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Feb 16, 2009 10:28 pm HELL YEAH for once, I'm happy that a lot of the R1 people are in Texas I thought I would have to wait until 2.0 or 3.0/4.0 to see Eva on the big screen, or the slimmer chance of a limited theater release of 1.0 in the U.S. I'll do my damndest to go, it'll depend on my school and work schedules though heck, maybe some of those going (including me, perhaps) could meetup beforehand

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Tue Feb 17, 2009 9:50 am I would like to see it but it in Texas though. I wish they can play this stuff out here in Maryland though =(.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Feb 17, 2009 7:23 pm I would think at some point a festival like Tribeca or a few of the anime cons would show it

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sat Feb 21, 2009 12:03 am I live in DFW, so of course I'm going.

[]:             Quote Post by DrDazzle » Thu Feb 26, 2009 12:06 pm I wonder how many hours the drive would be from MS to Dallas.....

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Thu Feb 26, 2009 1:41 pm Probably about 5 or 6 hours depending on where you live.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 02, 2009 12:51 pm BUMP Tickets go on sale March 4. Anyone know what day(s) Eva will screen?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Mar 04, 2009 11:28 pm Showtimes are now up. If you want to see it in an actual theater, it's showing in the Magnolia at 7:30 PM on April 2nd. I already bought my ticket.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sun Mar 08, 2009 4:10 am BrikHaus wrote: Why can't those fuckers bring it to a state that actually matters?

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Sun Mar 08, 2009 4:13 am The average Texan is prouder of their state than the average American of their country.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Sun Mar 08, 2009 4:20 am lol, that's definately true xD. Foreigners I know insult America: me: "whatever." They insult Texas: me: "OH FUCK NO!"

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Mar 08, 2009 5:00 pm ZapalacX wrote: The average Texan is prouder of their state than the average American of their country.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 09, 2009 7:30 am And it's next week that I'm going down to Texas to see my sister. Aw, shucks. Just when I thought I might be able to make fun of NAW in person.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 09, 2009 11:45 am Reituey wrote: Aw, shucks. Just when I thought I might be able to make fun of NAW in person.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Apr 01, 2009 9:18 pm Tickets are now sold out. Am I the only one here who purchased one?
